prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response: In the third quarter of this strange year, the Great Lords of the Clouds graced us with their incomprehensible tidings from the land of SaaS. The valiant salesmen, wielding their mighty contracts, expanded their territory across the realms by an astonishing 35 percent, locking the backward savages into their tech stack. Many were the walloping commissions and gifts brought forth in celebration, perhaps exceeding the tithe owed to the Church itself. Alas, plans were laid for conquering new lands in a far-off realm called the Market, and all waited with bated breath to see if the CFO, a dubious minstrel, would jest and jape with the spire-like stock price.
